etymology.description: 樣(樣) (yàng, "appearance; pattern") + 板(板) (bǎn, "board; template") + 間(間) (jiān, "room; between") combine to describe a space designed to show a template or example layout, hence 'showroom'. The idea is that the room (間) represents a pattern (樣板) that can be replicated or followed.
etymology.interpretation: 樣(樣) (yàng, "appearance; pattern") suggests an outward appearance or model form. 板(板) (bǎn, "board; template") implies a standard or mold that can be followed. 間(間) (jiān, "room; between") refers to a physical space. Together, this forms the concept of a room set up to display a model for others to replicate, embodying the notion of a showroom.

examples.chinese: 我们参观了样板间(我們參觀了樣板間)。
装修公司提供了几个样板间(裝修公司提供了幾個樣板間)，供客户参考(供客戶參考)。
examples.pinyin: Zhuāngxiū gōngsī tígōngle jǐ gè yàngbǎnjiān, gōng kè hù cānkǎo.
examples.english: The renovation company provided several showrooms for clients to reference.

examples.chinese: 新的样板间展示了最新的家居设计(新的樣板間展示了最新的家居設計)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn de yàngbǎnjiān zhǎnshìle zuìxīn de jiājū shèjì.
examples.english: The new showroom displayed the latest home designs.
